source_test: HEC.FDA.ModelTest/unittests/AlternativeTest.cs (AlternativeResults_Test), HEC.FDA.ModelTest/unittests/AlternativeComparisonReportTests.cs (ComputeEqad), HEC.FDA.ModelTest/integrationtests/DefaultDataComputeOutcomes.cs (WithoutAnalytical_ScenarioResults) -- Phase 6 Task 11 capstone: the FULL simulated pipeline, Scenario.Compute -> Alternative.AnnualizationCompute -> AlternativeComparisonReport.ComputeAlternativeComparisonReport, built from impact-area simulations (not direct ScenarioResults/AlternativeResults construction like alternative.json/alternative_comparison_report.json use) -- proving the whole chain end to end.
note: Three case `kind`s, all dispatched by run_end_to_end/EvalEndToEnd, all reusing build_simulation/BuildSimulation (the same construct shape every simulation-target fixture uses: impact_area_id, flow_frequency, flow_stage, stage_damage, optional levee) so no new simulation-construction glue is needed -- only the outer Scenario->Alternative->Report wiring is new. Every UncertainPairedData construct (flow_stage/levee, not just stage_damage) carries a non-null damage_category/asset_category -- mirrors the upstream tests' own reuse of ONE shared CurveMetaData object across flow_stage AND stage_damage (e.g. AlternativeTest.cs's `metaData` field), and matters structurally, not just cosmetically: ImpactAreaScenarioSimulation.PopulateRandomNumbers only calls GenerateRandomNumbers for a curve whose CurveMetaData.IsNull is false (ImpactAreaScenarioSimulation.cs:277) -- a flow_stage left with the default null CurveMetaData silently skips random-number population and null-refs on first SamplePairedData call under a SEEDED compute (harmless for the deterministic annualization/comparison_report cases below, fatal for the seeded muncie case, so all three kinds tag it consistently). `annualization` (AlternativeResults_Test): builds base_construct/future_construct (3-node StageXs={0,150000,300000} stage-damage on a 2-node Uniform flow-stage over Uniform(0,100000,1000) flow frequency, future damages scaled by futureDamageFractionOfExistingDamage), computes each via a single-impact-area Scenario with cc(iterations,iterations) and computeIsDeterministic=true, then Alternative::annualization_compute(discount_rate, period_of_analysis, alternative_id, &base, &future, base_year, future_year). Asserts sample_mean_eqad/eqad_exceeded_with_probability_q(0.5)/sample_mean_base_year_ead/sample_mean_future_year_ead/base_year_ead_exceeded_with_probability_q(0.5)/future_year_ead_exceeded_with_probability_q(0.5) -- all PIN, cross-checked against the upstream InlineData literals {208213.8061 (futYr 2072, frac 2.0), 239260.1814 (futYr 2050, frac 2.0), 150000 (frac 1.0, base==future so EAD==EqAD, both futYr rows)} within the test's own 0.01 relative tolerance. `comparison_report` (AlternativeComparisonReportTests.ComputeEqad): builds without-project (no levee) and with-project (levee: Deterministic(0) below top, Deterministic(1) at top, StageXs={0,15,30}, top_of_levee_elevation=7.5) base/future constructs, computes each via its own single-impact-area Scenario(cc(1,iterations), deterministic), annualizes both sides via annualization_compute, then AlternativeComparisonReport::compute_alternative_comparison_report(without, [with]). Asserts eqad_reduced_exceeded_with_probability_q(0.5)/sample_mean_base_year_ead_reduced/sample_mean_future_year_ead_reduced -- PIN, cross-checked against the upstream InlineData literals {51442 (futYr 2072), 59410 (futYr 2050), base-year-reduced 36500, future-year-reduced 75000} within the test's own 0.1 relative tolerance -- plus two EXACT cross-check pairs (each PIN'd independently, verify identical by inspection, matching the C# test's own Assert.Equal(...) with no tolerance): report.sample_mean_without_project_base_year_ead == the without-project AlternativeResults' own sample_mean_base_year_ead, and report.sample_mean_with_project_eqad == the with-project AlternativeResults' own sample_mean_eqad. `muncie` (DefaultDataComputeOutcomes.WithoutAnalytical_ScenarioResults): the Muncie synthetic default-curve-data integration test -- LogPearsonIII(3.707,.24,-.475,48) flow frequency, a 17-point Triangular stage-discharge curve, and TWO 40-point Normal stage-damage curves (Residential, Commercial), computed via a single-impact-area Scenario with cc(50000,50000) SEEDED (computeIsDeterministic=false, matching the upstream test's default `ConvergenceCriteria(maxIterations: 50000)` which leaves minIterations at its own 50000 default, so min==max==50000 -- an exactly-fixed, non-convergence-dependent iteration count), asserting ScenarioResults::sample_mean_expected_annual_consequences(damage_category) for Commercial/Residential -- PIN, cross-checked against the upstream literals {310937.1 commercial, 295506.53 residential} within the test's own loose 0.11 relative tolerance (a Monte Carlo smoke test, not a tight pin, per the upstream test's own tolerance). Measured full-suite ctest runtime with this case included stayed under ~10s, so NO iteration-count reduction was needed -- the full upstream 50000 is used verbatim. Only WithoutAnalytical_ScenarioResults is ported; the sibling AnalyticalWithRegUnreg_ScenarioResults test requires WithInflowOutflow (a regulated/unregulated flow transform the simulation builder does not yet expose), out of scope for this capstone per the task brief's 'keep ONE representative Muncie case' allowance. All `expected: "PIN"` values are gate-captured against the real, patched C# Scenario/Alternative/AlternativeComparisonReport, never hand-derived; the literals named above are cross-checks against the upstream test's own (looser) InlineData/expected values, not the fixture's actual pinned literals.
